text:
There once was an old bookstore owner named Olivia. She loved books. So much so that after retiring she opened a bookstore just for her favorites.

Every evening she retired early to indulge herself in tales of romance and adventure.

One rainy evening she picked out a book about an adventurer. She sat alone reading.

In time she felt a flutter in her stomach. She heard footsteps... but only her own.

She jumped. There was a small gray bat. She waved her finger.

The bat fluttered closer.

She opened an Ancient Oak just outside her window.

There was nothing special about the ancient.

She whispered.

That one?

She sighed deeply.

Every summer. That's why I've always loved them.

The next one she found out about was extraordinary.

Just one.

She paused.

That's you.

She smiled.

Oh! Okay!

She turned the pages quickly.

Next.

She heard fluttering inside the next oak.

She peeked.

An owl!

She stared at him.

Where?

Outside
